name: Apocalyptic Adventure: Episode 1
short_description: SURVIVE. STRATEGIZE. DEFEND. An intense VR Survival Tower Defense experience. Scavenge for resources, fortify your base, craft deadly traps, and fight to survive against relentless hordes.
detailed_description:
SURVIVE. STRATEGIZE. DEFEND.

The horde is coming. Your only hope is your wits, your hands, and the scrap you can find. Welcome to Apocalyptic Adventure, an intense VR Survival Tower Defense experience where every piece of junk matters and every second counts.

Forget abstract menus and god-like views; here, you are on the ground, desperately turning scavenged junk into an arsenal of deadly traps and fortifications. It's not just about surviving; it's about outsmarting the horde.

SCAVENGE & CRAFT FOR YOUR LIFE

Ransack your environment for vital resources. A rusty can, a discarded battery, a broken tool; these are not trash, they are the building blocks of your survival. Immerse yourself in a hostile world where you must actively search for materials, then use what you find at your workbench to craft everything from simple barricades and traps to melee tools and weapons.

BUILD YOUR DEFENSE

The enemy is relentless, but you can be clever. Don't just fight, outsmart. Analyze your surroundings and fortify your position. Board up defenses and lay a gauntlet of traps for the unsuspecting horde. Every decision and every nail can mean the difference between surviving and being overrun.

FIGHT WITH ANYTHING YOU CAN FIND

You are on the front lines. When your defenses fail and the horde breaks through, it's time to get your hands dirty. Use a mix of firearms, melee weapons, and crafted explosives to fight back directly, defending the fortifications you built with your own hands. With limited ammo, you'll learn that a well-placed trap is worth more than a dozen bullets.

A PASSION-DRIVEN VR EXPERIENCE

Developed by a solo creator, Apocalyptic Adventure: Episode 1 is built from the ground up for a comfortable and immersive room-scale VR experience. Featuring both teleportation and smooth motion support, this is the pilot episode of a planned series, now rebuilt and refined in Unreal Engine 5 with community feedback to provide a stronger foundation for the future.
